scope: Mechanism probe for actual-LI compressed dKV: selected-key dot-K contraction with per-seq-tile partial rows instead of direct global grad_kv atomics.
read: This branch keeps the fast selected-key tl.dot body from dot_k_atomic, but writes into a dense [seq_tile, compressed_group, D] partial slab using load/store, then reduces partials across seq tiles. It removes final cross-tile global dKV atomics but still writes the full raw KxD contribution volume. The result closes the cross-tile-contention hypothesis: S4096 emit-only is about the same as dot_k_atomic, and full partial+reduce is slower.
decision: Do not pursue dense per-seq-tile partial slabs as the production write path. They remove cross-tile global atomics, but S4096 emit-only remains at the dot_k_atomic wall because raw KxD contribution writes are still 1.0x. The next kernel must keep selected-key MFMA/dot-K contraction and reduce repeated compressed-group contributions inside the tile before global memory, or fuse LI/compressor gradients so fewer dKV rows are emitted.
